tasks.subject: runtime-neutral-context-channel [P1] Add excludeDynamicSections to Claude system prompt
tasks.description:
Add `excludeDynamicSections: true` to the Claude adapter's `sdkOptions.systemPrompt` so the `claude_code` preset stops injecting its native working-directory / auto-memory / git sections. DorkOS's own `<git_status>` block (still produced today by `buildPerMessageContext` → `buildGitBlock`) then becomes the single source of truth, ending the per-turn double-injection of git status (preset native git + DorkOS body block). This phase maps to Linear DOR-132 and is independently shippable AHEAD of the rest of the spec — NO interface changes, NO new files.

FILE TO TOUCH: `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/message-sender.ts`.

The current `sdkOptions.systemPrompt` (around line 265) is:
```ts
systemPrompt: {
  type: 'preset',
  preset: 'claude_code',
  append: systemPromptAppend,
},
```
Change it to:
```ts
systemPrompt: {
  type: 'preset',
  preset: 'claude_code',
  append: systemPromptAppend,
  // Suppress the preset's native working-directory/auto-memory/git sections so
  // DorkOS's own server-derived <git_status> block is the single source of truth.
  // Ends the per-turn double-injection of git status (ADR-0273 decision A2).
  excludeDynamicSections: true,
},
```

VERIFICATION GATES (resolve during this task and record findings in the PR/Linear comment):
- G1 — `excludeDynamicSections` re-injection under per-resume: confirm whether the SDK re-injects the stripped sections into EVERY resumed turn's first message (potential session-start stale git beside our fresh block) or only at true session start. Decide tolerate-vs-compensate. ADR-0273 notes the SDK re-injects stripped sections into the first user message, so a session-start git snapshot may briefly coexist with DorkOS's fresh per-turn block (minor, stale-tolerant). Document the observed behavior.
- G2 — env duplication: confirm whether `excludeDynamicSections` suppresses the preset `<env>` block or only cwd/memory/git. If the preset env survives, note it — DorkOS's own `<env>` block comes from `buildEnvBlock` in `context-builder.ts` (via `systemPrompt.append`); if both appear, reconcile so env is not doubled. Document the finding (it feeds Phase 5's env-entry decision).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Satisfies AC3): Git status is injected exactly once per turn (no preset + body duplicate), server-derived, identical format. Verify by inspecting a real turn's prompt (or the SDK input) on a multi-turn session: exactly ONE git block reaches the model. Tests: extend/add a test under `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/__tests__/` asserting the built `sdkOptions.systemPrompt` carries `excludeDynamicSections: true`. Tests written and passing; `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Adding excludeDynamicSections to Claude system prompt

tasks.subject: runtime-neutral-context-channel [P2] Create shared additional-context module (types, CONTEXT_TAG, Zod schemas)
runtime-neutral-context-channel [P2] Wire ClientContextSchema into SendMessageRequestSchema (drop standalone uiState)
tasks.description:
Wire the neutral client context into the wire contract. FILE TO TOUCH: `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`.

The current `SendMessageRequestSchema` (around line 174) is:
```ts
export const SendMessageRequestSchema = z
  .object({
    content: z.string().min(1, 'content is required'),
    cwd: z.string().optional(),
    correlationId: z.string().uuid().optional(),
    clientMessageId: z.string().optional(),
    /** Client UI state snapshot — validated against UiStateSchema via z.lazy (forward ref). */
    uiState: z.lazy(() => UiStateSchema).optional(),
    runtime: z.string().optional(),
    agentPath: z.string().optional(),
  })
  .openapi('SendMessageRequest');
```

Change it to ADD `context: ClientContextSchema.optional()` and REMOVE the standalone `uiState` field (repo standard: no legacy parallel field — `uiState` now lives inside `context.uiState`):
```ts
export const SendMessageRequestSchema = z
  .object({
    content: z.string().min(1, 'content is required'),
    cwd: z.string().optional(),
    correlationId: z.string().uuid().optional(),
    clientMessageId: z.string().optional(),
    /** Neutral client-sourced context signals (ui_state, queued). Server derives git_status/env. */
    context: z.lazy(() => ClientContextSchema).optional(),
    runtime: z.string().optional(),
    agentPath: z.string().optional(),
  })
  .openapi('SendMessageRequest');
```
Import `ClientContextSchema` from the additional-context module (or use `z.lazy` if it lives in this same file to avoid ordering issues). Keep the inferred `SendMessageRequest` type export.

UPDATE THE ROUTE that destructures the parsed body: `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` line ~344 currently does `const { content, cwd, uiState, runtime: runtimeHint, agentPath } = parsed.data;` and passes `uiState` to `triggerTurn` at line ~377. Change it to destructure `context` instead of `uiState` and pass `context` to `triggerTurn` (the `triggerTurn`/assembler signature changes land in Phase 3 task 3.3 — coordinate the field name `context` so they meet). For THIS task, if Phase 3 has not landed yet, temporarily map `context?.uiState` into the existing `uiState` arg so the build stays green, and leave a clearly-marked `// TODO(phase-3): pass full context bag` — but PREFER ordering so 3.3 lands together. (Dependency wiring: this task depends on 2.1; task 3.3 depends on this.)

Rebuild shared: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build`.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: `SendMessageRequestSchema` accepts `{ content, context: { uiState, queued: true } }` and REJECTS a top-level `uiState` field (it is gone). `pnpm typecheck` finds and forces an update at every former `uiState` reader (route + tests). Tests: update `apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/sessions-multi-runtime.test.ts` and any schema test to use `context` instead of `uiState`. Tests written and passing; lint/typecheck clean.

tasks.subject: runtime-neutral-context-channel [P3] Thread context through trigger-turn + embedded-turn-trigger to the assembler
tasks.description:
Replace the `uiState` thread with the neutral `context` → assembler → `additionalContext` flow in both turn-trigger paths. The assembler must consult `runtime.getCapabilities().nativeContext` and omit accordingly.

FILE: `apps/server/src/services/session/trigger-turn.ts`.
- `TriggerTurnOpts` (around line 104) currently has `uiState?: UiState;`. Replace with `context?: ClientContext;`.
- The deps port type (`TriggerTurnDeps`) declares `sendMessage(sessionId, content, opts: MessageOpts)` (line ~90) and must also expose the active runtime's capabilities for the assembler's `nativeContext`. Add a dep `getCapabilities(): RuntimeCapabilities` (or pass `nativeContext` in) — wire it from the route where the runtime is already resolved.
- Around line 139 (`const { sessionId, clientId, content, cwd, uiState, projector, deps } = opts;`) destructure `context` instead of `uiState`.
- Around line 192, replace `deps.sendMessage(sessionId, content, { cwd, uiState })` with:
```ts
const additionalContext = await assembleAdditionalContext({
  cwd: cwd ?? '',
  clientContext: context,
  nativeContext: deps.getCapabilities().nativeContext,
});
const tapped = tapEachEvent(
  deps.sendMessage(sessionId, content, { cwd, additionalContext }),
  () => { signalFirstEvent(); tryRekey(); }
);
```
  Resolve the correct `cwd` (the assembler needs the effective cwd; if `cwd` is undefined the existing chain in the adapter resolves it — pass the same `cwd` the trigger already has, and let `getGitStatus` no-op gracefully for a missing/invalid dir, returning `{ isRepo: false }`).

FILE: `apps/server/src/services/session/embedded-turn-trigger.ts`.
- It declares `uiState?: UiState` (line ~29), destructures it in `trigger({ ..., uiState })` (line ~45), forwards it (line ~57), and calls `sendMessage: (sid, text, opts) => runtime.sendMessage(sid, text, opts)` (line ~63). Replace `uiState` with `context?: ClientContext`, run it through `assembleAdditionalContext` using the embedded runtime's `getCapabilities().nativeContext`, and forward `{ cwd, additionalContext }`.

FILE: `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts`.
- Pass the parsed `context` (from task 2.2) into `triggerTurn({ …, context })` (replacing the `uiState` arg at line ~377), and provide the `getCapabilities`/`nativeContext` dep from the resolved runtime.

FILE: `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct/services.ts` and `direct/session-methods.ts` — the DirectTransport's `turnTrigger.trigger` currently receives `uiState: options?.uiState` (session-methods line ~149). That client-side change is task 4.x; for THIS server task, ensure the embedded `trigger` accepts `context` so the client task can pass it.

Import `assembleAdditionalContext` from `../session/context-assembler.js` (or relative within session/). Import `ClientContext`/`RuntimeCapabilities` from `@dorkos/shared/additional-context` and `@dorkos/shared/agent-runtime`.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Satisfies AC1, AC6, AC7): A turn started via `triggerTurn` calls `sendMessage` with `{ cwd, additionalContext }` and NO `uiState`; `content` is passed through pristine (unmutated). The assembler is consulted and `nativeContext` omission is honored end-to-end. Tests: update/add `apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/trigger-turn.test.ts` (and embedded equivalent) asserting `sendMessage` receives `additionalContext` (a bag) and that `content` equals the input bytes. The render-leak and pristine-content integration assertions are in Phase 6 (task 6.1). Tests written and passing; lint/typecheck clean.
tasks.activeForm: Threading context through the turn-trigger paths to the assembler

runtime-neutral-context-channel [P6] Integration tests: pristine content over SSE + clean rendered transcript + command dispatch unaffected
tasks.description:
End-to-end integration coverage proving the channel is correct: pristine `content` reaches the runtime, the rendered transcript shows no injected tags, and command dispatch is unaffected. Use supertest + `collectSseEvents` from `@dorkos/test-utils` and `FakeAgentRuntime`/`testScenarios` per `.claude/rules/testing.md`. Rebuild `@dorkos/shared` before running (stale-dist false-red).

LOCATION: `apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/` (alongside `sessions-multi-runtime.test.ts`) and/or `apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/`.

Scenarios to assert:
1) PRISTINE CONTENT (Satisfies AC1): POST a plain message via `SendMessageRequestSchema` body `{ content: 'hello world', context: { uiState } }`. Capture what `sendMessage` receives (FakeAgentRuntime spy or capture the `additionalContext`-bearing opts). Assert the `content` arg equals exactly `'hello world'` (byte-for-byte) and the `additionalContext` bag is delivered out-of-band (the ui_state rides the bag, not `content`). AC1 text: "Sending a message never mutates content: the JSONL user record's text (post-strip) equals the bytes the user typed, for plain, queued, and command messages."
2) QUEUED MESSAGE (Satisfies AC2): POST `{ content: 'queued text', context: { queued: true } }`. Assert the assembled bag contains a `queue_note` entry, and that a transcript-parser render of the resulting user record (after `stripSystemTags`) shows `queued text` with NO `<queue_note>` and NO `[Note: …]` prose. AC2 text: "Queued messages: the model receives a <queue_note> block; the rendered transcript shows the pristine user text with no [Note: …] prose."
3) COMMAND DISPATCH (Satisfies AC4): POST `{ content: '/compact' }` to a session whose known commands include `/compact`. Assert NO context is prepended (the prompt reaching the runtime is the bare trimmed `/compact`) and the command still dispatches. AC4 text: "Slash commands still dispatch (/compact, /context) — the command-skip guard holds; context is not prepended on command turns."
4) CLEAN RENDERED TRANSCRIPT: a turn with ui_state + queue signal yields a rendered transcript with no `CONTEXT_TAG` blocks (relies on task 5.2's strip).

Use `collectSseEvents(app, sessionId, content)` where appropriate to drive the durable stream end-to-end; for content-arg capture prefer a `FakeAgentRuntime` whose `sendMessage` records the `(content, opts)` it was called with.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: All four scenarios pass. Specifically AC1 (plain + queued + command content unmutated), AC2 (queue_note delivered, transcript clean of note prose), AC4 (command guard holds). Tests written and passing; `pnpm test -- --run` green (NOT bare `pnpm vitest run` — DEV-env gotcha); lint/typecheck clean.
tasks.activeForm: Writing integration tests for pristine content and command dispatch

tasks.subject: runtime-neutral-context-channel [P6] Unit round-trip + cache validation gate (G3)
tasks.description:
Unit-level round-trip coverage and the empirical cache-validation gate.

ROUND-TRIP UNIT TESTS (Satisfies AC5; complements the parametrized strip guard from task 5.2): `renderContextEntry` ↔ `stripSystemTags` round-trip for each `ContextKind`. For each kind, build a representative entry, render it via `renderContextEntry`, embed the result in a user-message string, run `stripSystemTags`, and assert the injected block is gone (no tag survives). Confirm `CONTEXT_TAG` is exhaustive over `ContextKind` (the `satisfies Record<ContextKind, string>` check plus a runtime key-count assertion). Place under `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/__tests__/` and/or `packages/shared/src/__tests__/`. AC5 text: "stripSystemTags strips every CONTEXT_TAG value; a test proves no injected tag renders as user content; adding a new ContextKind requires no separate strip edit."

SCHEMA VALIDATION + QUEUE SIGNAL UNIT TESTS: assert `ClientContextSchema` accepts `{ uiState, queued: true }` and the queue signal maps to a `queue_note` entry through the assembler (overlaps task 3.2 but asserted here at the schema boundary). Assert `SendMessageRequestSchema` rejects a top-level `uiState`.

CACHE VALIDATION GATE G3 (Satisfies AC8): empirically confirm the change preserves prompt-cache hits on a multi-turn session (token-burn is cache RE-creation churn, not duplicate sends — see MEMORY `project_token_burn_diagnosis`). Measure cache hit-rate before/after on a real multi-turn session (the JSONL `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` vs `cache_creation_input_tokens` across turns is the signal). Because the system prompt is now fully static (excludeDynamicSections + env via append) and per-turn context sits AFTER the cache breakpoint in the user message, hits should be preserved or improved. Document the measured before/after hit-rate in the PR/Linear comment. AC8 text: "No regression in prompt-cache hit rate on a multi-turn session (compare before/after)." If a real-session measurement is impractical in CI, record the manual measurement steps and the observed numbers in the task comment, and add an assertion-light test documenting the cache-correctness invariant (static system prompt, per-turn context in user message).

VERIFICATION GATES to close out and record findings for: G3 (cache, above). Re-confirm G1 (excludeDynamicSections re-injection — from task 1.1) and G2 (env duplication — from task 1.1/3.2) are resolved and documented. G4 (future hook option) stays deferred — note it is NOT in scope.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Round-trip tests pass for every `ContextKind`; schema tests pass; the cache hit-rate before/after is measured and documented showing no regression (AC8). Tests written and passing; `pnpm test -- --run` green; lint/typecheck clean.
tasks.activeForm: Writing round-trip unit tests and validating the prompt cache
